workers.description: Number of workers, corresponding to the number of nodes (or machines) that will be used to deploy the model

hfToken.description: Huggingface access token, only required to load gated model weights

workingMemory.description: Temporary storage to use to cache model weights (in GB), should be big enough to hold the model weights. All workers should have enough free disk to accommodate the weights.

memory.description: Memory to be used per single worker (final one = memory * workers). Workers should have these many GB of memory available.

sharedMemory.description: Shared memory to be used per single worker (final one = shared_memory * workers). Workers should have these many GB of shared memory available.

cpus.description: CPUs to be used per single worker (final one = cpus * workers). Workers should have these many CPUs available.

gpus.description: GPUs to be used per single worker (final one = gpus * workers). Workers should have these many GPUs available.

imagePullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the containers (Always, IfNotPresent, Never)

restartPolicy.description: Restart policy for the containers (Always, OnFailure, Never)

forceVersion.description: Force a specific PyPI vLLM engine version to deploy (overrides auto-detection). Eg. 0.17.1

extra.description: Extra arguments to pass to the vLLM engine

toolCallParser.description: Tool call parser to use for the model

modelNameOverride.description: Model name to use (defaults to model_id) during inference

loraModules.description: LoRa adaptor(s) to load; semi-colon separated list of huggingface repos of the lora adaptors to load

templateUrl.description: URL to the template to use for the model

enabled.description: Enable ingress for the vLLM service
Enable LMCache for caching generated tokens

maxLocalCpuSize.description: LMCache: Maximum CPU cache size in GB. See https://docs.lmcache.ai/api_reference/configurations.html

localCpu.description: LMCache: whether or not to use CPU offloading. See https://docs.lmcache.ai/api_reference/configurations.html

maxLocalDiskSize.description: LMCache: Maximum disk cache size in GB. See https://docs.lmcache.ai/api_reference/configurations.html

localDisk.description: LMCache: Path to local disk cache. Format: “file:///path/to/cache” or null. See https://docs.lmcache.ai/api_reference/configurations.html

remoteUrl.description: LMCache: Remote storage URL. Format: “protocol://host:port” or null. See https://docs.lmcache.ai/api_reference/configurations.html

baseUrl.description: Base URL of the LiteLLM service (central model registry). This can be an external LiteLLM instance or an internal deployment in the pool (default)

key.description: Master key of the LiteLLM service (central model registry)

accessGroup.description: What access group to assign the model to (ringfencing)

kalavaiExtras.description: Extra information to store in the LiteLLM model registration (must be a dictionary)

waitReady.description: Wait for the model to be ready before registering it in LiteLLM

url.description: Base URL of the Lago service (payment system)

apiKey.description: Master key of the Lago service

externalSubscriptionId.description: ID of the Lago subscription to send events to

eventCode.description: Event code to use when submitting event to Lago payment API

eventInterval.description: Interval in seconds to send the lago event

envFromSecrets.description: Dictionary mapping environment variable names to secret names

queue.description: Queue to use for the deployment

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName to use for the deployment

jobId.description: Job ID to label spawned objects.

nodeSelectors.description: target specific devices. Expected format -> key: value pairs, where 'value' is a list of elements

nodeSelectorsOps.description: Logical operation to use to apply to nodeSelectors. Accepted values: 'OR', 'AND'

nodeAffinityPreferenceKey.description: Node affinity preference key to use for the deployment

workingGpuMemory.description: Working GPU memory to be used per single worker (final one = working_gpu_memory * workers). Workers should have these many GB of working GPU memory available.
